title: Tapping for the Marriage Drift
subtitle: A five-minute tapping practice for the distance that arrived without a clear moment
excerpt: A five-minute tapping practice for the relationship drift that happens gradually and is hard to name. The script addresses the grief of changed closeness, the fear of bringing it up, and the permission to reach without knowing what will come back.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. The EFT tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique, developed in the mid-1990s.

practice.whenToUse: Use when the distance in a relationship has been building and you have not yet found a way to name it. Good before a conversation you are avoiding, or on any day when the gap feels bigger than usual.

content.text: A five-minute tapping practice for relationship drift. The script addresses the distance that builds over time without a clear cause, the grief of missing someone who is still there, and the permission to make a small reach without needing to fix everything at once.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop (repeat x3)
Even though we are not as close as we used to be and I am not sure how to close that distance, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though the drift happened gradually and now it feels like a lot of ground to recover, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I am grieving who we were while living with who we have become, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Tapping round
**Eyebrow:** We have drifted.
**Side of eye:** I am not sure when it happened.
**Under eye:** The distance between us is real.
**Under nose:** We are living parallel lives in the same house.
**Chin:** I miss them, and I do not know how to say that.
**Collarbone:** The gap feels bigger than one conversation can fix.
**Under arm:** I am afraid to bring it up in case nothing changes.
**Top of head:** We used to be closer than this.
Reframe to positive
**Eyebrow:** Drift is not the end. It is a gap, and gaps can close.
**Side of eye:** One small reach is enough to start.
**Under eye:** I do not need to fix the whole distance this week.
**Under nose:** I can say something small and true.
**Chin:** Closeness is built in small moves, not one big conversation.
**Collarbone:** I can reach without knowing what they will do.
**Under arm:** Wanting to close the gap is enough to start with.
**Top of head:** We can come back to each other.
Tap the round three times. Then tap the reframe three times.
Where this practice comes from
Original to homemade.education. EFT tapping was developed by Gary Craig in the 1990s.
